0.q: a LysR-type transcriptional regulator, HhmR, represses the DgcY activity through direct physical interaction. Interestingly, the presence of c-di-GMP further promoted the complex formation of DgcY-HhmR

1.q: This interaction, mediated by the regulatory domain of HhmR, obviously reduced DgcY activity, especially in the presence of c-di-GMP.

2.q: These data confirmed that DgcY is functional in c-di-GMP synthesis and its associated phenotypes.

3.q: DgcY 202–406 exhibited high activity, while the heat-inactivated DgcY 202–406 completely lost its ability to synthesize c-di-GMP.

4.q: DgcY 202–406 exhibited high activity, while the heat-inactivated DgcY 202–406 completely lost its ability to synthesize c-di-GMP.
